characters.physical_details: 6'1", lean sprinter's build with unnaturally perfect bilateral symmetry. Synthetic grafts in both legs, spinal neural bridge, visual enhancement. After Ch 5: facial scars, healed right-shoulder dislocation, and a left wrist that healed fast but aches permanently.

key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: engineer-athlete trust damaged by the archive discovery
key_relationships.margot_voss: confrontational after her treatment of Lena is revealed
key_relationships.tomas_reyes: senior athlete Kai feels responsible for after Tomas's injury

characters.literacy_languages: English (native). Basic Spanish from high school. No other languages.

state_transitions.change: Injured: dislocated right shoulder, broken left wrist, facial lacerations from blackout crash
Begins private override training — testing conscious control of synthetic muscle autonomous firing
Discovers Yusuf's archive; relationship with Yusuf fractures
Competes unpatched; virus triggers during race; finishes last under own power
In Lausanne post-Games; commentator offer pending; undecided

characters.availability: Present throughout; primary POV in Ch 1, 5, 7, 11, 15, and 17. In medical recovery during Ch 6-7.

characters.physical_details: 5'10", swimmer's build with broad shoulders and long torso. Neural link port visible at the base of her skull. During desync, her expression flattens and her eye focus changes.

key_relationships.margot_voss: coach whose concealment betrays her trust
key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: engineer who installs her patch; professional trust
key_relationships.dara_lund: training partner; possible future under continued optimization

characters.occupation_status: Professional Apex Games swimmer (400m, 800m freestyle), Meridian developmental program athlete since birth
characters.literacy_languages: German (native), Russian (fluent, parents' language), English (fluent, slightly formal register), French (functional — Swiss schooling).

state_transitions.change: Desync becomes legible — motor control and cognitive symptoms
Desync severity revealed to be far worse than reported; loses segments of conscious experience
Receives Yusuf's partial patch — desync stabilizes but sensory acuity blunted, reaction times slower, silence where system processing was
Competes on the patch; places 3rd in 400m freestyle
Training at a smaller facility in Montreux, independent of Meridian's program
Brief legacy stroke corrections still surface, but she can detect them early and stop

characters.availability: Present throughout; primary POV in Ch 2, 10, 11, 13, and 17.

characters.physical_details: 5'9", slight, dark-haired, usually rumpled. Wears ordinary prescription glasses and has notably steady hands.

key_relationships.kai_brennan: professional relationship fractured by archive discovery in Ch 14
key_relationships.lena_morozova: professional trust — he installs her patch
key_relationships.petra_engel: professional alliance built on institutional distrust
key_relationships.farah_qadir: younger sister with Voss-Kessler syndrome; his primary personal motivation
key_relationships.margot_voss: professional colleagues; he designs the patch that would sever her feed access

characters.occupation_status: Lead calibration engineer, Kirchberg Institute. PhD from Imperial College London in biomechanical systems.
characters.literacy_languages: English (native, Birmingham dialect), Urdu (fluent, family language), Arabic (reading proficiency). Technical literacy in multiple programming and firmware languages.

state_transitions.change: Finds Meridian signatures in virus code; begins building alternative patch
Reveals Farah's condition to the group; trust complicated rather than earned
Archive discovered by Kai; confrontation; reveals Meridian conspiracy suspicion
Publishes Meridian analysis; investigation triggered

characters.availability: Present throughout; primary POV in Ch 3 and 9.

characters.physical_details: 5'8", former swimmer's build, heavier than in her competition years. Her neural coaching interface is personal wrist-mounted equipment, not facility-issued.

key_relationships.tomas_reyes: coach-athlete; she pushes him dangerously; his paralysis is partly her consequence
key_relationships.petra_engel: medical superior who reviews the feed logs and suspends her

characters.occupation_status: Neural performance coach at Kirchberg Institute. Former professional swimmer (5th place, 2028 Olympics).

Conceals Lena's desync severity to preserve feed access
Experiences Tomas's crash through the feed at full sensory resolution
Suspended from coaching; feed access revoked; addiction exposed
Develops intermittent phantom leg deadness from Tomas's crash through the feed
In neural addiction treatment facility in Munich

characters.availability: Present through Ch 16; absent from Doha in Ch 17; in Munich treatment by Ch 18.

characters.physical_details: Tall, angular, grey-streaked, clinically precise, chronically tired-looking.

key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: professional alliance; she covers for his unauthorized work
key_relationships.margot_voss: medical oversight; she discovers and acts on Margot's feed abuse

characters.occupation_status: Medical director, Kirchberg Institute. Previously at a Meridian facility in Stuttgart where an athlete died from enhancement rejection.

Reviews Margot's feed logs; suspends her from coaching

characters.availability: At Kirchberg through Ch 16; absent from Doha.

characters.physical_details: 5'11", expressive, balanced decathlete build. Three years into enhancement, still less fully calibrated than older athletes.

key_relationships.kai_brennan: idolizes Kai as a senior athlete who made the jump from natural to enhanced

characters.occupation_status: Professional Apex Games decathlete. First Games. Meridian-sponsored. Family in Quito dependent on sponsorship income.

Receives Meridian's patch after signing the data contract
Virus-triggered blackout during hurdles; spinal impact; paralyzed from waist down; neural bridge destroyed; career ended

key_relationships.lena_morozova: Lena invites her to the Ch 12 argument; professional respect
key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: opposes his alternative patch from genuine conviction

characters.occupation_status: Athlete liaison, Meridian BioSystems. Corporate representative but not a corporate cynic — driven by her brother's death in 2034.

Argues against Yusuf's alternative from position of genuine conviction

characters.availability: Present from Ch 8 in Meridian-delegation scenes.

key_relationships.lena_morozova: training partner; swimming same events; represents Lena's possible future

characters.occupation_status: Professional Apex Games swimmer (400m, 800m freestyle). Bergen Performance Institute program.
characters.availability: Present at Kirchberg throughout; mainly visible in Ch 2 and Ch 13.

characters.physical_details: Seen only on video call. Dark hair; Voss-Kessler appears in slowed speech and occasional word-finding difficulty.

key_relationships.yusuf_qadir: older brother; his primary personal motivation

characters.occupation_status: Lives in Birmingham. Employed status unclear — the degenerative condition is progressive.

timeline.story_start: Late January 2038 — winter in the Bernese Alps, six months before the Apex Games opening ceremony
timeline.estimated_span: Approximately seven months: late January through mid-August 2038

seasonal_track.season: Late winter — deep snow, short days, mostly indoor life.
Late winter into early spring — lower-elevation snowmelt and longer days.
Spring thaw — mud, runoff, and longer light.
Late spring/early summer — compressed departure period before Doha.
Late July Doha — 40°C+ outside, air-conditioned inside.
August — summer in Lausanne, Montreux, and Munich.

key_events.event: First sensory blackout — Kai's crash on the indoor track
Meridian delegation arrives with patch offer
key_events.timing: Approximately 1 week after blackout (3 weeks after arrival)

key_events.event: Yusuf publishes his Meridian code-signature analysis and a de-identified archive extract; public inquiries begin

geography.primary_setting: The Kirchberg Institute is a concrete-and-glass training and calibration campus in the Bernese Alps above Meiringen, about 2,100m up, with track, pool, medical wing, quarters, cafeteria, and admin spaces.

key_locations.name: Calibration Bay
key_locations.details: Cold, sterile room of rigs, monitors, and constant processor hum; Yusuf's main workspace.

key_locations.name: Indoor Track
key_locations.details: Altitude-controlled 200m indoor oval with rubber surface, echoing footfalls, and the padded curve wall Kai hits in Ch 5.

key_locations.name: Olympic Pool
key_locations.details: 50m ten-lane pool at 27°C with constant chlorine smell and a glass-walled observation deck.

key_locations.name: Medical Bay
key_locations.details: Clinical wing with imaging, surgery, recovery rooms, and direct access to calibration.

key_locations.name: Cafeteria
key_locations.details: Central communal room with long tables, protein-heavy institutional food, and mountain-facing windows.

key_locations.name: Athletes' Quarters
key_locations.details: Single rooms with bed, desk, bath, and a wall display of enhancement diagnostics.

key_locations.name: Neural Coaching Suite
key_locations.details: Small room beside training spaces with coach/athlete stations and wall monitors for feed sessions.

key_locations.name: Doha Apex Games Venue
key_locations.details: Air-conditioned Doha complex of glass and steel built for global broadcast. Ch 17 only.

key_locations.name: Centre Sportif, Montreux
key_locations.details: Small 25m municipal pool where Lena trains independently in Ch 18.

key_locations.name: NeuroBalance Treatment Facility, Munich
key_locations.details: Schwabing residential treatment center for neural-interface addiction; Margot is here in Ch 18.

distances: Kirchberg Institute to Meiringen (nearest town): approximately 30 minutes by road, single mountain road
Kirchberg Institute to Zurich: approximately 2 hours by road
Kirchberg Institute to Doha Apex Games venue: international flight, roughly 6 hours
Calibration Bay to Indoor Track: same building, adjacent wings, approximately 3 minutes' walk through a connecting corridor
Athletes' Quarters to Cafeteria: same campus, approximately 5 minutes' walk
Lausanne to Montreux: approximately 30 minutes by train along Lake Geneva

world_rules: The Apex Games operate under separate governance from the Traditional Olympic Games. Both competitions occur in the same year but at different venues.
Bio-integrative enhancement is legal in the Apex Games: neural links, synthetic muscle, engineered bone, sensory augments, and spinal bridges.
Enhancement systems need periodic shared-network calibration; that network is the virus vector.
Neural coaching feeds let coaches receive an athlete's sensation in real time; the link also opens a return channel that can transmit data and virus exposure.

Enhancement subsystems can act without conscious input: injury-preventing muscle fire, motor optimization, and sensory pre-filtering. Users notice them mainly when they malfunction or get ahead of intent.
Voss-Kessler syndrome is a rare progressive neural disease, unrelated to enhancement, that begins with speech and fine-motor loss and can progress into cognition.
The virus corrupts sensory-processing firmware, causing 10-60 second blackouts while autonomous systems may keep the body moving.
Meridian BioSystems dominates enhancement hardware, sponsorship, and calibration infrastructure, including Kirchberg funding.
The data contract gives Meridian permanent, transferable rights to all biometric, genetic, neural, and physiological data produced by or through the enhancement system, including baseline biology.
Yusuf's compartmentalized patch can stop viral/desync progression without surrendering data, but it permanently severs the neural coaching feed and reduces optimization performance by roughly 10-15%.
Meridian's validated stability patch preserves full optimization, but it does not guarantee safety; Tomas blacks out and is paralyzed after taking it.

power_structure.mechanism: Sponsorship contracts, proprietary hardware, network control, and the data contract.

power_structure.over: Athletes and staff at the facility
power_structure.mechanism: Authority over schedules, access, and operations, limited by Meridian funding.
Medical-director authority to ground athletes, order exams, and suspend staff.
Technical control of calibration and firmware.
Feed access to athlete sensation and pacing parameters.

objects.name: Yusuf's encrypted personal archive
objects.origin: Personal device brought to the facility
objects.status: Active through Ch 14, then discovered by Kai. Partly published in Ch 18.

objects.name: Margot's feed interface
objects.origin: Personal coaching equipment, not facility-issued. Wrist-mounted device that connects to the athlete's neural system.
objects.status: Active through Ch 15, then access revoked by Petra.

objects.name: Meridian data surrender contract
objects.origin: Produced by Meridian legal team in response to the virus
objects.status: Presented Ch 8. Most athletes sign. Kai, Lena, and a few holdouts refuse.

objects.name: Meridian stability patch
objects.origin: Offered with the data-surrender contract in Ch 8
objects.status: Installed in athletes who sign. Preserves optimization, but Tomas still suffers a virus-triggered blackout and paralysis in Ch 14.

objects.name: Kai's wrist brace
objects.status: Worn through Ch 7-8; the wrist keeps aching after it heals too fast.

objects.name: Yusuf's partial patch
objects.origin: Developed by Yusuf in the calibration bay, Ch 9-12
objects.status: Installed in Lena Ch 13. Other holdout athletes receive it during compressed time of Ch 16. Kai refuses it.

objects.name: Yusuf's public virus-analysis release
objects.origin: Published to public repositories in Ch 18 from his private analysis and a de-identified archive extract
objects.status: Public by Ch 18; triggers scrutiny of Meridian, an 11% stock drop, and outside access to the released data.

income_sources.amount: Variable by athlete tier. Tomas's contract includes family support provisions that make refusal economically devastating.
income_sources.reliability: Dependent on continued Apex Games participation and compliance with Meridian's terms. The data contract adds a new compliance layer.

income_sources.amount: Not specified, but the facility's operations depend on it.
income_sources.reliability: Institutional — the facility cannot operate independently of Meridian's support, which constrains the administration's willingness to challenge Meridian's decisions.

knowledge_state.knows: The virus may be deliberately engineered by Meridian — structural signatures in the code match Meridian's development tools
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone until Ch 14, when he tells Kai

knowledge_state.knows: He is collecting unauthorized biological data from athletes for his sister's treatment research
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone except Petra's suspicions; Kai learns in Ch 14; the group gets only the Farah explanation in Ch 12

knowledge_state.knows: Lena's desync is far more severe than reported — she loses entire segments of conscious experience
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Lena suspects; others do not know until Petra reviews the logs in Ch 15

knowledge_state.knows: The neural coaching feed may be a virus transmission vector
She systematically pushes athletes' heart rates past safe margins during feed sessions for personal gratification
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone until Petra reviews the telemetry in Ch 15

knowledge_state.knows: Yusuf is conducting unauthorized after-hours work in the calibration bay
Margot kept using the feed after identifying it as a virus vector and systematically drove athletes above prescribed heart-rate zones
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Facility administration until Petra's review; athletes do not see the full pattern

knowledge_state.knows: Her brother died from enhancement cascade failure in 2034
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Not hidden; she says it publicly in Ch 8

knowledge_state.knows: His proprioception scores show system-initiated micro-movements ahead of conscious intent
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Not hidden, but Kai discusses it openly only in Ch 11 with Lena

knowledge_state.knows: Yusuf copied athletes' raw biometric and neural data into a private archive during calibration sessions
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Lena and most athletes until part of the archive is exposed in Ch 18

environmental_constants: Kirchberg sits at 2,100m; new arrivals feel the thin air.
The calibration bay has a constant low processor hum.
The pool smells of chlorine, and Lena carries it in her hair.
The indoor track smells of rubber and echoes footfalls.
Mountain weather stays visible through the glass: snow, thaw, then spring.
Room displays glow with enhancement diagnostics at night.
Lena often touches the port at the base of her skull.
Kai's left wrist aches after healing too fast.
Lighting is mostly artificial: fluorescent in clinical spaces, warmer in residential ones.
Doha is the Alps' opposite: heat, humidity, and air-conditioning shock.
